description: Cross-print triangle — three Riolu prints (Nat. Dex 447, Sinnoh Gen IV Fighting-type Emanation Pokemon, Lucario's pre-evolution) across Sun & Moon Burning Shadows (Saya Tsuruta, sunset beach, aura-sensing flavor), Sword & Shield Vivid Voltage (ryoma uratsuka, autumn forest, stamina flavor), and Sword & Shield Brilliant Stars (Teeziro, daytime field, identical stamina flavor to VIV). All three mutually edged: BRS↔BUS, BUS↔VIV, BRS↔VIV. Triangle topology — structurally satisfying, three nodes all reference each other, the rarest and most respectful shape. Mirrors the wave 28 E4 Snubbull cross-print triangle pattern. The Fates Collide Riolu (no. 46) exists in the corpus but is NOT enriched (tags_hub empty, needs_manual_review: true) and is correctly excluded from the triangle pending vision-pass enrichment.
other_candidates_considered: Future Lucario line character node — Riolu (Nat. Dex 447) + Lucario (Nat. Dex 448) is one of the franchise's most-printed evolutionary lines. Surface as Nodeley candidate when a Lucario (or Mega Lucario, or Lucario V/VMAX/VSTAR/ex) lands in corpus. Trigger threshold: 1 Lucario print + the existing 3 Riolu prints = 4-member line, line-node-worthy under the Charizard-line precedent. Currently 3/4 (Riolu only, no Lucario).

Fates Collide Riolu (no. 46) — corpus member but un-enriched (no vision, no tags, manual_review flag). When that card gets a vision pass it should be promoted into this triangle, expanding it to a 4-print fan or a tetrahedron. Edgelord defers until vision-passed.
